topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification flashed across my screen, a familiar chime that usually signaled homework reminders, but this time, it was different. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison, my notoriously strict history teacher. My breath hitched. I clicked it open, expecting a critique of my essay, but the image attached was a candid photo taken outside the school library—a slightly blurred shot of him leaning against a stone pillar, sunlight catching the sharp line of his jaw. A slow warmth began to spread through my chest, a heavy, pleasant pressure that made my palms sweat. I stared at the picture, my gaze lingering on the way his shirt stretched across his chest. A flush crept up my neck, and I found myself biting the inside of my cheek, a small, involuntary sound escaping my lips. It felt like a secret shared, a dangerous current running beneath my skin.

[story 2]

I was scrolling aimlessly through my feed when I saw it: a post from Ms. Evans, my English teacher. It was a picture of her at a weekend market, laughing, her hair slightly disheveled, radiating a warmth I hadn't noticed before. Then I saw the little ‘like’ icon appear, followed by a direct message. Her words were brief—a comment on a poem I’d shared weeks ago—but the context of the follow request sent a startling jolt through me. My heart started hammering against my ribs, a frantic rhythm that felt entirely too loud in the quiet room. I leaned closer to the monitor, my pupils dilating as I took in the casual intimacy of the connection. A shiver traced its way down my spine, and I felt a distinct tightening in my lower abdomen, a sudden, intense focus that made everything else fade away.

[story 3]

It was late, and I was procrastinating, the blue light of my laptop illuminating my face. Suddenly, a notification popped up—Mr. Davies had tagged me in a story. I clicked, expecting a simple acknowledgment, but the accompanying video showed him walking across campus, his silhouette framed against the twilight sky. The way the camera moved, capturing the subtle tension in his shoulders, made my entire body feel suddenly hyper-aware. A deep, slow burn ignited in my core. I couldn't look away; I traced the outline of his profile on the screen with my finger, the sensation sending a wave of heat radiating outward. My breathing became shallow, uneven. I felt a compelling urge to reach out, to bridge the distance between the digital screen and the man whose casual presence now felt overwhelmingly potent.
